can you please rewrite with fluency this text: In September 2022, it was reported that the color of the radiators at the service of the ABB/Hitachi transformer 1LIT756009.01 was fading. The radiators are varnished with Mega Wickens paint, blue RAL 5012. The issue was reviewed and confirmed through a joint visit with the customer on site on March 21, 2023.

In September 2022, there were reports indicating the gradual fading of the radiators' color in relation to the ABB/Hitachi transformer 1LIT756009.01. The radiators had been originally coated with Mega Wickens paint, specifically in the shade of blue RAL 5012. To investigate and verify this matter, a joint visit was conducted with the customer at the site on March 21, 2023.
also this text: The investigation concluded that the phenomenon occurs gradually when a surface painted with a varnish whose pot life has been exceeded is exposed to sunlight.
Upon investigation, it was determined that the observed phenomenon occurs gradually when a surface coated with varnish, whose pot life has surpassed its recommended duration, is exposed to sunlight.
Also this: This final layer is applied to radiators with painting cycle already concluded and dried, with only purpose to provide unifomity of colour to all the units of a transformer.
This final layer is applied to radiators that have already completed the painting cycle and dried, with the sole purpose of ensuring a uniform color across all units of a transformer.
And this: This means that the corrosion and protection system of the radiators is not affected, and that the issue is aesthetic only.
This signifies that the corrosion and protection system of the radiators remains unaffected, and that the issue is purely aesthetic in nature.

Please summarize: The phenomenon could have originated from an excess of hardener during the application of the last painting layer, which preceded the shipment of the radiators to the Hitachi transformer factory.
This final layer is applied to radiators with the painting cycle already concluded and dried, with the only purpose of providing uniformity of color to all the units of a transformer.
This signifies that the corrosion and protection systems of the radiators remain unaffected and that the issue is purely aesthetic in nature.

The fading color of the radiators at the Hitachi transformer factory was likely caused by an excess of hardener during the application of the final painting layer. This layer is added after the painting process is complete and is solely intended to ensure consistent color across all transformer units. It is important to note that this fading is only a cosmetic issue and does not impact the corrosion or protection systems of the radiators.

Please summarize: In September 2022, there were reports indicating the gradual fading of the radiators’ color in relation to the ABB/Hitachi transformer 1LIT756009.01. The radiators had been originally coated with Mega Wickens paint, specifically in the shade of blue RAL 5012. To investigate and verify this matter, a joint visit was conducted with the customer at the site on March 21, 2023. 
Upon investigation, it was determined that the observed phenomenon occurs gradually when a surface coated with varnish, whose pot life has surpassed its recommended duration, is exposed to sunlight. This final layer is applied to radiators that have already completed the painting cycle and dried, with the sole purpose of ensuring a uniform color across all units of a transformer.

In September 2022, reports emerged of the radiators' color fading gradually in relation to the ABB/Hitachi transformer 1LIT756009.01. A joint visit was conducted on March 21, 2023 to investigate the matter. The investigation revealed that the fading occurs over time when a varnish-coated surface exceeds its recommended pot life and is exposed to sunlight. The final layer, applied to radiators after the painting process, serves the sole purpose of achieving color uniformity among all transformer units.

please rewrite fluently: In September 2022, there were reports indicating the gradual fading of the radiators’ color in relation to the ABB/Hitachi transformer 1LIT756009.01. The radiators had been originally coated with Mega Wickens paint, specifically in the shade of blue RAL 5012. To investigate and verify this matter, a joint visit was conducted with the customer at the site on March 21, 2023. 
Upon investigation, it was determined that the observed phenomenon occurs gradually when a surface coated with varnish, whose pot life has surpassed its recommended duration, is exposed to sunlight. This final layer is applied to radiators that have already completed the painting cycle and dried, with the sole purpose of ensuring a uniform color across all units of a transformer.

Reports in September 2022 raised concerns about the radiators of the ABB/Hitachi transformer 1LIT756009.01, as their color was observed to fade progressively. Initially, the radiators had been coated with Mega Wickens paint, specifically in a blue hue known as RAL 5012. To address these reports, a visit to the site was organized on March 21, 2023, allowing for a joint investigation with the customer.

After thorough examination, it was concluded that the fading phenomenon arises gradually when a surface previously coated with varnish exceeds its recommended pot life and is exposed to sunlight. Notably, this final layer is applied to radiators that have already completed the painting cycle and fully dried, with its main objective being to ensure a consistent color across all transformer units. Importantly, it is worth highlighting that this problem solely affects the aesthetics of the radiators, with no impact on their corrosion resistance or protection systems.
Also: In light of this, apart from the aesthetic defect, we confirm the correct application of the cycle, fully endorsing the warranty on the painting as agreed between the parties.

Based on the findings, it is confirmed that apart from the aesthetic defect, the painting cycle was correctly applied, thereby fully upholding the agreed warranty on the painting between the parties involved.
Please put well the following: Mega Wilckens nevertheless is available to rectify the paint issue applying a further varnish hand on site
Mega Wilckens, however, is willing to address the paint issue by applying an additional varnish layer on-site to rectify the problem.
translate in english: se il cliente non è soddisfatto, nonstante quanto sopra, Mega Wilckens è disponibile a sistemare il problema applicando una mano di vernice direttamente in sito
If the customer is not satisfied, despite the aforementioned, Mega Wilckens is available to fix the issue by applying a coat of paint directly on-site.
please use a fluent english here: If the customer is not satisfied, despite the aforementioned, Mega Wilckens is available to fix the issue by applying a coat of paint directly on-site.

In the event that the customer remains unsatisfied, despite the aforementioned details, Mega Wilckens is prepared to address the matter by directly applying a fresh coat of paint on-site to rectify the issue.
Synonyms of aforementioned?
Some synonyms of "aforementioned" are "aforenamed," "above-mentioned," "previously stated," and "formerly mentioned."
